ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus DreamJob / AeroBlade DLL side-loading via Microsoft-signed LOLBINs from user-writable paths
ucs.description: Hunts the hallmark Lazarus (G0032 / Diamond Sleet / ZINC / Labyrinth Chollima) loader pattern reported across Operation DreamJob, AeroBlade, LightlessCan and the Mandiant/CrowdStrike/Microsoft tracking of MagicRAT and ForestTiger droppers: a legitimately Microsoft-signed but rarely-invoked helper EXE (colorcpl.exe, CameraSettingsUIHost.exe, wsmprovhost.exe, iexpress.exe, CompMgmtLauncher.exe, dfsvc.exe, etc.) is dropped into %APPDATA%, %TEMP% or \Users\Public alongside a malicious co-located DLL (colorui.dll, version.dll, msvcr100.dll, wsmprovhost.dll, etc.) which it side-loads. This pair-shap
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("colorcpl.exe","CameraSettingsUIHost.exe","CompMgmtLauncher.exe","dfsvc.exe","iexpress.exe","wsmprovhost.exe","fodhelper.exe","computerdefaults.exe","dccw.exe") (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Downloads\\*") NOT Processes.process_path="C:\\Windows\\*" by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_id [ | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count values(Filesystem.file_name) as sideloaded_dll values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as dll_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` ] | where like(dll_path, process_path."%") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
// Microsoft-signed helper binaries repeatedly abused in DreamJob/AeroBlade/LightlessCan loaders
let LazarusSideloadHosts = dynamic([
    "colorcpl.exe","CameraSettingsUIHost.exe","CompMgmtLauncher.exe",
    "dfsvc.exe","iexpress.exe","wsmprovhost.exe","fodhelper.exe",
    "computerdefaults.exe","dccw.exe","fixmapi.exe","msconfig.exe"
]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LazarusSideloadHosts)
// host EXE is running from a user-writable path, NOT System32
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Users\Public\")
// DLL co-located with the EXE = classic side-load shape
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
// host EXE itself is genuinely signed by Microsoft
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has "Microsoft"
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
// drop the obvious noise from legit installers staging signed Microsoft DLLs
| where not(FileName in~ ("mscoree.dll","msvcp140.dll","vcruntime140.dll","ucrtbase.dll"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          SignedHost  = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          HostPath    = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          HostSHA256  = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          SideLoadedDLL = FileName,
          DLLPath     = FolderPath,
          DLLSHA256   = SHA256,
          Grandparent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let LazarusSideloadHosts = dynamic([
    "colorcpl.exe","CameraSettingsUIHost.exe","CompMgmtLauncher.exe",
    "dfsvc.exe","iexpress.exe","wsmprovhost.exe","fodhelper.exe",
    "computerdefaults.exe","dccw.exe","fixmapi.exe","msconfig.exe"
]);
// Stage 1 - Microsoft-signed helper binary launched from a user-writable directory
let HostExec = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where BinName in~ (LazarusSideloadHosts)
    | where not(NewProcessName startswith @"C:\Windows\")
    | where NewProcessName has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Users\Public\")
    | extend HostFolder = strcat_array(array_slice(split(NewProcessName, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\")
    | project ExecTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, BinName, NewProcessName, HostFolder, NewProcessId, ParentProcessName, CommandLine;
// Stage 2 - non-Microsoft DLL written into the same folder shortly before/after
HostExec
| join kind=inner (
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
    | project FileTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName, TargetFileSHA256, ActingProcessName
  ) on $left.Computer == $right.DvcHostname
| where FileTime between (ExecTime - 1h .. ExecTime + 5m)
| where TargetFilePath startswith HostFolder
| project ExecTime, FileTime, Computer, Account, BinName, NewProcessName, ParentProcessName,
          SideLoadedDLL = TargetFileName, DLLPath = TargetFilePath, DLLSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256,
          DLLDropper = ActingProcessName
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus DreamJob DLL Side-Loading via Microsoft-Signed Helper Binary
id: 9c8a4b2e-5f7d-4a13-b8c2-7a01e9f3d6c4
status: experimental
description: Detects the Lazarus Group (Diamond Sleet / ZINC / Labyrinth Chollima) DreamJob, AeroBlade and LightlessCan loader pattern - a Microsoft-signed but rarely-invoked helper binary executed from a user-writable directory, used as a vehicle for DLL side-loading of malicious code (colorcpl.exe + colorui.dll, CameraSettingsUIHost.exe + DUI70.dll variants, wsmprovhost.exe + custom payload DLLs).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0032/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/en/eset-research/lazarus-luring-employees-trojanized-coding-challenges-case-spanish-aerospace-company/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.defense-evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1218
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0032
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_lolbin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\colorcpl.exe'
      - '\CameraSettingsUIHost.exe'
      - '\CompMgmtLauncher.exe'
      - '\dfsvc.exe'
      - '\iexpress.exe'
      - '\wsmprovhost.exe'
      - '\fodhelper.exe'
      - '\computerdefaults.exe'
      - '\dccw.exe'
      - '\fixmapi.exe'
  selection_unusual_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Downloads\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
  filter_system:
    Image|startswith: 'C:\Windows\'
  condition: selection_lolbin and selection_unusual_path and not filter_system
falsepositives:
  - Portable installers/repacks staging signed Microsoft helpers from %TEMP%
  - Software-distribution platforms relocating signed binaries into %APPDATA%
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic 'DLL side-loading' detections fire constantly on legitimate installers and game launchers. What makes this Lazarus-specific is the *exact host inventory* - colorcpl.exe, CameraSettingsUIHost.exe, wsmprovhost.exe, dfsvc.exe etc. are essentially never invoked from %APPDATA% or %TEMP% by legitimate software, but appear repeatedly in Mandiant, ESET, Microsoft (Diamond Sleet/ZINC), Kaspersky an

_field_issues.message: `InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName` is not a column of DeviceImageLoadEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents.
`InitiatingProcessParentFileName` is not a column of DeviceImageLoadEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents, DeviceRegistryEvents, DeviceEvents.

ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus C2 staging via legitimate cloud storage by Microsoft-signed LOLBIN (BLINDINGCAN / MagicRAT / TraderTraitor pattern)
ucs.description: Hunts Lazarus's well-documented preference (T1102.002 - Bidirectional Web Service / T1071.001 / T1567.002) for tunnelling C2 and staging payloads through legitimate Tier-1 cloud services (Dropbox API, OneDrive Graph, GitHub raw, Cloudflare Workers, Notion) - heavily seen in BLINDINGCAN, MagicRAT, AppleJeus and TraderTraitor (Diamond Sleet). The actor-specific twist: the process making that cloud call is one of the Microsoft-signed helper binaries from the side-loading set above, OR rundll32/regsvr32 launched from a non-System32 path - never a browser or Office product. This pair (LOLBIN + Tier
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.app) as app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","dl.dropboxusercontent.com","graph.microsoft.com","api.onedrive.com","raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com","*.workers.dev","*.web.app","api.notion.com") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_name All_Traffic.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | where process_name IN ("colorcpl.exe","CameraSettingsUIHost.exe","wsmprovhost.exe","dfsvc.exe","iexpress.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe") AND NOT match(process_path, "^C:\\\\Windows\\\\System32\\\\") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
// LOLBINs Lazarus has used as the calling process for cloud-hosted C2/staging
let LazarusBins = dynamic([
    "colorcpl.exe","CameraSettingsUIHost.exe","wsmprovhost.exe",
    "CompMgmtLauncher.exe","dfsvc.exe","iexpress.exe",
    "rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe",
    "curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","powershell.exe"
]);
// Tier-1 cloud services repeatedly seen in Lazarus C2 reporting
let CloudC2Domains = dynamic([
    "api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","dl.dropboxusercontent.com",
    "graph.microsoft.com","api.onedrive.com","onedrive.live.com",
    "raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com",
    "api.notion.com","pastebin.com","controlc.com"
]);
// 30-day baseline of (process,domain) pairs seen elsewhere in the org
let Baseline = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LazarusBins)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (CloudC2Domains)
       or RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.(workers\.dev|web\.app|trycloudflare\.com)$"
    | summarize by InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteUrl;
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LazarusBins)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where RemoteUrl has_any (CloudC2Domains)
   or RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.(workers\.dev|web\.app|trycloudflare\.com)$"
// LOLBIN must be running from somewhere unusual - real system processes don't talk to Dropbox
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\")
   or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", "-EncodedCommand", "DownloadString", "IEX")
// strip everything that's already established in the env
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteUrl
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          AccountName  = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Process      = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ProcessPath  = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ProcessCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ProcessSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort,
          ParentImage  = InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let LazarusBins = dynamic([
    "colorcpl.exe","CameraSettingsUIHost.exe","wsmprovhost.exe",
    "CompMgmtLauncher.exe","dfsvc.exe","iexpress.exe",
    "rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe",
    "curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","powershell.exe"
]);
let CloudC2Domains = dynamic([
    "api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","dl.dropboxusercontent.com",
    "graph.microsoft.com","api.onedrive.com","onedrive.live.com",
    "raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com",
    "api.notion.com","pastebin.com","controlc.com"
]);
let Baseline = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | extend procName = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where procName in~ (LazarusBins)
    | where DstHostname has_any (CloudC2Domains) or Url has_any (CloudC2Domains)
    | summarize by procName, DstHostname;
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| extend procName = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where procName in~ (LazarusBins)
| where DstHostname has_any (CloudC2Domains)
   or DstHostname matches regex @"(?i)\.(workers\.dev|web\.app|trycloudflare\.com)$"
   or Url has_any (CloudC2Domains)
| where ActingProcessName !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\"
   or ActingProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", "-EncodedCommand", "DownloadString", "IEX")
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on procName, DstHostname
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          procName, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine,
          DstHostname, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Lazarus has a distinctive operational pattern of routing C2 / payload retrieval through reputable cloud providers (BLINDINGCAN -> Dropbox API, MagicRAT -> compromised legit websites, TraderTraitor -> GitHub raw, AppleJeus -> Cloudflare Workers) precisely to avoid TLS-inspection-based reputation blocks - documented in CISA AA20-239A, Mandiant M-Trends and Microsoft's Diamond Sleet write-ups. The di

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActorUsername` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImAuthentication, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
